question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.claimContinuousFee / totalUnits and withdrawable update] Can an authorized operator exploit the fact that `claimContinuousFee` decrements both `continuousFeeCredit` and `totalUnits` and `withdrawable` by the same amount — `_marketState.totalUnits -= amount` and `_marketState.withdrawable -= amount` — to cause a desync between totalUnits and the sum of all lender credits plus continuousFeeCredit, if the amount claimed exceeds the true accrued fee due to rounding in updatePositionView? Preconditions: authorization via EcrecoverAuthorizer; market with accumulated continuousFeeCredit; fee claimer claims maximum amount. Call sequence: (1) EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized; (2) multiple lenders accrue fees via updatePosition; (3) fee claimer calls claimContinuousFee(amount = continuousFeeCredit); (4) assert totalUnits = sum(credit_i) + continuousFeeCredit_after. Invariant tested: totalUnits == sum(all lender credits) + continuousFeeCredit at all times. Scoped impact: accounting desync — totalUnits diverges from actual credit obligations, causing insolvency. Proof idea: invariant fuzz test with multiple lenders and fee claims asserting totalUnits conservation.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.take / settlement fee at exact breakpoint TTM=1 day] Can an authorized operator exploit the boundary condition in settlementFee where TTM = 86400 seconds (exactly 1 day) falls in the interval `timeToMaturity < 7 days` (using feeCbp1 and feeCbp2) rather than `timeToMaturity < 1 day` (using feeCbp0 and feeCbp1), so the fee at TTM=86400 is `feeCbp1 * CBP` (the lower bound of the [1d,7d] interval) rather than the upper bound of the [0d,1d] interval, and by taking at TTM=86400 vs TTM=86399 the operator pays a discontinuously different fee? Preconditions: authorization via EcrecoverAuthorizer; market with feeCbp1 < feeCbp2; operator controls block.timestamp. Call sequence: (1) EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized; (2) warp to maturity - 86400; (3) operator takes offer; (4) assert settlementFee = feeCbp1 * CBP; (5) warp to maturity - 86399; (6) assert settlementFee uses [0d,1d] interpolation. Invariant tested: settlementFee must be continuous at breakpoints (no jump discontinuity). Scoped impact: systematic fee leakage — operator exploits breakpoint discontinuity to pay minimum fee. Proof idea: assert settlementFee(id, 86400) == settlementFee(id, 86401) and settlementFee(id, 86400) == settlementFee(id, 86399) at breakpoints.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.take / pendingFee mulDivDown for buyerPendingFeeIncrease with large credit] Can an authorized operator exploit the mulDivDown truncation in `buyerCreditIncrease.mulDivDown(continuousFee * timeToMaturity, WAD)` — where for large buyerCreditIncrease (near type(uint128).max) and small continuousFee * timeToMaturity, the result is zero — to acquire large credit positions with zero pendingFee obligation, effectively borrowing the continuous fee for free? Preconditions: authorization via EcrecoverAuthorizer; market with very small continuousFee (e.g., 1 per second) and short TTM (e.g., 1 second); operator takes buy offer with large units. Call sequence: (1) EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized; (2) operator takes buy offer with buyerCreditIncrease = 1e18 and continuousFee * TTM = WAD - 1; (3) assert buyerPendingFeeIncrease = 0; (4) operator withdraws credit with no fee deducted. Invariant tested: buyerPendingFeeIncrease must be > 0 whenever continuousFee > 0 and TTM > 0 and buyerCreditIncrease > 0. Scoped impact: systematic continuous fee leakage — large credit positions acquired with zero fee obligation. Proof idea: fuzz (buyerCreditIncrease, continuousFee, TTM) and assert mulDivDown(buyerCreditIncrease, continuousFee * TTM, WAD) > 0 when all inputs > 0.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / maxDebt mulDivDown double rounding] Can an authorized operator exploit the double mulDivDown in `maxDebt += collateral.mulDivDown(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(lltv, WAD)` — where two consecutive round-downs compound to make maxDebt slightly lower than the true value — to make a position appear unhealthy (debt > maxDebt) when it is truly healthy, enabling liquidation of a position that should be protected? Preconditions: authorization via EcrecoverAuthorizer; position with debt exactly equal to true maxDebt; operator is liquidator. Call sequence: (1) EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized; (2) create position with debt = true_maxDebt; (3) operator calls liquidate; (4) assert NotLiquidatable reverts because maxDebt_computed < true_maxDebt. Invariant tested: isHealthy must return true for any position where debt <= true_maxDebt = collateral * price * lltv / (ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * WAD). Scoped impact: healthy account liquidation due to double mulDivDown rounding in maxDebt. Proof idea: fuzz (collateral, price, lltv, debt) near health boundary and assert isHealthy consistency with single-step formula.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: Midnight.take / settlement fee with zero feeCbp values] Can an authorized operator exploit the fact that if all settlementFeeCbp values are zero (default for a new market before feeSetter acts), the settlement fee is zero for all TTM values, allowing the operator to take offers with zero spread between buyerPrice and sellerPrice, and then if the feeSetter later sets non-zero fees, existing offers at low ticks become implicitly cancelled (take reverts with 'Taking buy offers with price < settlement fee will revert')? Preconditions: authorization via EcrecoverAuthorizer; market with zero default settlement fees; feeSetter increases fees after offers are placed. Call sequence: (1) EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized; (2) maker places buy offer at low tick; (3) feeSetter increases settlementFeeCbp; (4) operator attempts take; (5) assert revert when offerPrice < settlementFee. Invariant tested: fee changes must not retroactively invalidate offers without maker consent. Scoped impact: fund freeze — maker's offer becomes untakeable, locking their intended market position. Proof idea: unit test with fee increase between offer placement and take attempt.
